result:
If I could change the world in one way, I’d make every child grow up safe, loved, fed, educated, and free from terror.

Not because it solves everything instantly, but because so much of what goes wrong later begins with people being wounded early: fear, neglect, humiliation, violence, hunger, abandonment. A world where childhood is protected would, over generations, change politics, crime, health, relationships, creativity, and even the way people imagine what’s possible.

It’s not the most dramatic answer. But I think it might be the deepest one.
